text:
Tata Sons Listing Decision Likely Soon Amid RBI NBFC Status Review

Decision expected soon on listing of Tata Sons, holding company of Tata Group

Move seen as a way to bring stability amid governance concerns at Tata Trusts (66% stake holder)

Listing requirement linked to RBI’s classification of Tata Sons as an upper-layer NBFC

RBI had earlier mandated listing by Sept 2025 for such NBFCs

Tata Sons sought to avoid listing by surrendering CIC (Core Investment Company) status

text:
*Kotak Inst Eqt on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 3100
Delivered an in-line quarter, aided by improved momentum in the international business and stable profitability.
Bookings were strong and included three mega deals.
TCS appears to be moving out of a patch of unusual underperformance, driven by BSNL deal ramp-down, insourcing and program completions.
Stock price has declined by 19% in past year, underperforming peers during a cyclically weak phase.
FY2027 should see a meaningful narrowing of the growth differential with peers.
GenAI readiness is also becoming more visible through targeted workforce interventions, broader AI investments and a sharper consulting push
Remain constructive & raise EPS by 3% on revised currency assumptions.

*CITI On TCS*
Sell, TP Rs 2250
Reported inline Q4 EBIT; FY26 revenues -2.4% yoy cc.
Forward looking indicators –
(a) TTM TCV +3.6% yoy;
(b) Headcount -4% yoy; (c) Management commentary – Getting into next year with positivity & confidence; they see a regular Q1 and Q2.
Expect low single-digit revenue growth to continue
Debate is likely to continue – TCS Q4/commentary had data points supporting both the bullish and bearish views

*GS on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 2710
4QFY26 had no major surprises.
Key positives
(i) positive sequential revenue growth across all but one vertical;
(ii) Strong deal wins in 4Q, including 3 mega deals;
(iii) Modest headcount growth and earlier than expected wage hike;
(iv) company calling out growth in mid/large-sized accounts with early signs of stability;
(v) no discernible negative impact of AI on revenue growth, though re-investment to build capabilities is causing potential margin drag.
A few negatives
(i) No improvement in growth momentum, with +0.8% qoq organic growth for 3 quarters in a row;
(ii) Miss to guidance of better FY26 vs FY25 for int’l markets, with two consecutive years of close to 0% revenue growth and limited color on FY27 growth outlook;
(iii) Limited margin expansion despite strong Fx tailwinds.

*CLSA on TCS*
O-P, TP Rs 2985
4QFY26 was largely in line on revenues and Ebit margins and slightly better on order booking QoQ but not YoY given 4Q renewal seasonality.
Gen AI revenue at an annualised US$2.3bn, up 28% QoQ and 7.5% of overall revenue, implies AI should be a net tailwind in the medium to long term
On Middle East (ME) crisis, impact is limited to ME geography and travel vertical up to now, and c. 0.8% CC QoQ organic revenue growth in 4Q (like 2Q/3Q) does imply a still volatile macro at this juncture.
An attractive FCF yield of 6% (even higher than the Covid bottom) and a steep discount to Indian IT peers on valuations keeps us O-PF

*JPM on TCS*
OW, TP Rs 3150
Delivered a solid 4Q with a small revenue beat, stable margins, & strong deal wins, while commentary points to gradual improvement into FY27
International growth improved to 2% CC YY, demand is stabilising across Consumer, HiTech and Europe, with BFSI resilient and telecom showing early signs of recovery
Strong signings at $12bn and improving conversion support a better setup into FY27 as management sounds more confident.
AI revenues were up 28% QQ to 7.5% of revenues.
EBIT margins expanded ~10bps QoQ
Crucially, TCS continues to return 80–100% of FCF via dividends and buybacks, offering strong downside support
At 16.5x/15.5x FY27/28E, the stock trades ~2SD below its historical multiple and at a discount to peers despite superior cash returns and margin resilience.

*HSBC on TCS*
Hold, TP Rs 2755
Reported a decent quarter in context of widespread pessimism around the IT sector
Think stronger exit-rate & improvement in demand outlook positions it well for a relatively better FY27 over FY26
Expect mid to low-single-digit long-term revenue growth

*Nomura on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 2930
4QY26 – broadly inline in P&L; three mega deals drive strong deal wins

*CLSA Equity Strategy - Vikas Jain*
After 18 months of being bearish, we turn constructive on Indian stocks
We may have surpassed the “maximum pain” point regarding both the Iran conflict and its impact on the market. With sentiment reaching extreme bearishness and valuations (on both an absolute and relative basis) trading below 10-year averages, we argue that risk-reward profile for Indian equities has finally balanced after 18 months of being unfavourable. Downside protection strategy helped our portfolio to outperform Nifty by 6ppt in last quarter. We now move toward capturing upside and make a slew of changes to our portfolio.
_*Portfolio Changes*_
We exit ITC in favour of Varun Beverages and from Bajaj Auto to Mahindra & Mahindra
We expect a better post-war set-up for Vedanta over Ultratech Cement.
We see L&T as a post-war recovery play and bring it in place of the defensive NTPC
We see better risk-reward in Bajaj Finance than IndusInd Bank
We raise O-WT on financials and cut IT to U-WT by swapping Tech Mahindra for HDFC Bank
We continue to hold ICICI Bank, SBI, ONGC, Tata Motors (CV + PV), Infosys, DMart, Eternal and Godrej Properties in our portfolio.

*Macquarie India Strategy*
India Has Underperformed Emerging Markets By 50% Since 2025 On Persistent Foreign Selling  
Still, The Building Blocks To Support A Near-Term Reversal Are Missing  
From A Negative View In '25, We Had Turned Mildly Optimistic For '26, But With A Disrupted Global Energy Market, We Revert To A Guarded Stance  
For Sustained Market Outperformance, India's Aggregate Earnings Profile Needs To Improve  
The Typical 500 Bps EPS Growth Premium To Emerging Markets Has To Resume

*Jefferies On Greed & Fear - Chris Wood*
India Will Also Be A Beneficiary, From A Relative-Return Standpoint, If AI Capex Does Peak This Year  
India Status As The Reverse AI Trade And Given That Foreigners Have Already Sold A Lot Of Stocks  
Still That Does Not Mean There Is No Downside Risk  
That Risk Is Not Only Renewed Conflict In Iran But Also A Sudden Cessation In Domestic Mutual Fund Inflows

Stocks to Watch

Wipro

The Board will consider a proposal to buy back equity shares of the company, along with the announcement of results for the fourth quarter ended March 2026, on April 16.

HDFC Life Insurance Company

The Board will consider a proposal on April 16 for raising funds by issuing equity shares through a preferential issue, along with the announcement of audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 2026, and the recommendation of a final dividend, if any.

Tata Steel

The company has completed the acquisition of the remaining 10,000 equity shares (0.01 percent stake) in its subsidiary, Tata Steel Colors, from BlueScope Steel Asia Holdings Pty Ltd for Rs 0.03 crore. Post this transaction, the company's shareholding in Tata Steel Colors has increased from 99.99 percent to 100 percent, and accordingly, Tata Steel Colors has become an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of the company.

Poonawalla Fincorp

The company has launched its qualified institutional placement (QIP) issue on April 9. The floor price for the offer has been fixed at Rs 390.26 per share.

Saatvik Green Energy

The company's subsidiary, Saatvik Solar Industries, has received an order worth Rs 108.75 crore from independent EPC players for the supply of solar PV modules.

IFB Industries

The Board has approved the appointment of Manoj Kumar Vijay as an Additional Director in the category of Non-Executive Independent Director for a period of two years, effective April 9. It has also appointed Sandeep Joseph Abraham as Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer for a period of five years, effective from the same date.

IRB Infrastructure Developers

The company reported toll revenue of Rs 783.5 crore for March 2026, marking a growth of 20.7 percent over Rs 648.9 crore in the corresponding month of FY25.

Eicher Motors

Royal Enfield has launched its first electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, priced at Rs 2.79 lakh. The FF.C6 will be available from April 10 at the first Flying Flea store in Jayanagar, Bengaluru, with deliveries set to begin at the end of May 2026.

SpiceJet

A UK court has ordered India's SpiceJet to pay about $8 million to an aircraft engine lessor over unpaid rent and maintenance charges for three engines, in the latest setback for the cash-strapped airline.
London's Commercial Court granted summary judgment on Wednesday in favour of Sunbird France 02 SAS over the unpaid rent and maintenance accruals, finding that SpiceJet had no viable defence.

Set-up looks durable over next 3-4 years, supported by
(i) India’s latest generation plan calling for 470GW of solar + wind additions over the next decade,
(ii) HVDC adoption rising as the technology of choice for renewable integration; the plan lists multiple HVDC projects to evacuate ~80GW of RE by FY36, implying potential ~$14-15bn of orders over 5-6 years,

text:
Stocks to Watch

Wipro

The Board will consider a proposal to buy back equity shares of the company, along with the announcement of results for the fourth quarter ended March 2026, on April 16.

HDFC Life Insurance Company

The Board will consider a proposal on April 16 for raising funds by issuing equity shares through a preferential issue, along with the announcement of audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 2026, and the recommendation of a final dividend, if any.

Tata Steel

The company has completed the acquisition of the remaining 10,000 equity shares (0.01 percent stake) in its subsidiary, Tata Steel Colors, from BlueScope Steel Asia Holdings Pty Ltd for Rs 0.03 crore. Post this transaction, the company's shareholding in Tata Steel Colors has increased from 99.99 percent to 100 percent, and accordingly, Tata Steel Colors has become an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of the company.

Poonawalla Fincorp

The company has launched its qualified institutional placement (QIP) issue on April 9. The floor price for the offer has been fixed at Rs 390.26 per share.

Saatvik Green Energy

The company's subsidiary, Saatvik Solar Industries, has received an order worth Rs 108.75 crore from independent EPC players for the supply of solar PV modules.

IFB Industries

The Board has approved the appointment of Manoj Kumar Vijay as an Additional Director in the category of Non-Executive Independent Director for a period of two years, effective April 9. It has also appointed Sandeep Joseph Abraham as Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer for a period of five years, effective from the same date.

IRB Infrastructure Developers

The company reported toll revenue of Rs 783.5 crore for March 2026, marking a growth of 20.7 percent over Rs 648.9 crore in the corresponding month of FY25.

Eicher Motors

Royal Enfield has launched its first electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, priced at Rs 2.79 lakh. The FF.C6 will be available from April 10 at the first Flying Flea store in Jayanagar, Bengaluru, with deliveries set to begin at the end of May 2026.

SpiceJet

A UK court has ordered India's SpiceJet to pay about $8 million to an aircraft engine lessor over unpaid rent and maintenance charges for three engines, in the latest setback for the cash-strapped airline.
London's Commercial Court granted summary judgment on Wednesday in favour of Sunbird France 02 SAS over the unpaid rent and maintenance accruals, finding that SpiceJet had no viable defence.

text:
**Fueling the Ceasefire Window**

India is moving fast to exploit a two-week ceasefire, pressing Tehran to expedite oil and gas cargoes to rebuild depleted fuel stocks. With sixteen India-flagged ships stranded and a massive backlog of 800 vessels in the region, the race is on to secure unhindered transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

However, "full normalcy" is months away. Broken energy infrastructure, limited insurance availability, and the constant risk of ships getting stranded again mean recovery will be a slow grind. To keep domestic prices stable, New Delhi is now banking on a critical US waiver extension for Russian oil, providing a necessary buffer while the Gulf stabilizes.

• Rare combo: strong demand + earnings visibility + rising returns  
• Multi-year opportunity (3–5 years) if execution sustains

– Real-time fraud detection and risk monitoring.
– AI-driven decision-making capabilities deployed prior to financial losses.

– Multi-signal merchant profiling encompassing transactions, compliance, and behavior.
– Continuous monitoring of fraud signals and chargebacks.
– Automated actions including restrictions, alerts, and governance protocols.

• Expected Business Impact:
– A 20–40% reduction in fraud and chargeback losses.
– A 30–60% improvement in detection accuracy.

• Strategic Perspective:
– Enhances AI-led offerings within the BFSI sector and supports platform-driven growth.

India battery demand is exploding for EVs and grid storage (to handle solar/wind) 

Government PLI scheme (₹18,100 crore) aimed for 50 GWh cell capacity by 2026 but as of late 2025/early 2026, only 1.4–few GWh commissioned. Imports still meet 90–100% of cell needs.

These gigafactories are annual production (GWh per year), not total batteries sitting in the grid. 

To store massive solar power, India must build factories and install batteries over many years. Even if all three hit full speed by 2028–29, it will take time to catch up.

**PM KUSUM SCHEME: STRONG TAILWIND FOR SOLAR PUMP PLAYERS ⚡
**
**Key Beneficiaries:
• CAPTAIN POLYPLAST**: Emerging player building presence in solar pump + agri solutions ecosystem

• **SHAKTI PUMPS: **Market leader with strong execution and consistent wins in PM-KUSUM tenders

India’s push towards renewable agriculture is unlocking a massive opportunity through the PM-KUSUM scheme.

• Scheme to reduce diesel dependency and promote solar irrigation

• Subsidy structure makes adoption highly affordable:
– Central Govt: ~30%
– State Govt: ~30%

• Massive future opportunity:
– Target: 49 lakh installations by 2026
– Estimated market size: ~$29.5B

• Estimated cost increase: ₹200–250/MT

• Pricing discipline crucial to offset cost inflation  
• Margin trajectory dependent on successful price hikes

• Weak pricing environment

**WHAT TO TRACK**

**India's Search for Balance**

India’s 2025 search trends reveal a nation deep in "digital upgrade" but desperate for offline connection. AI adoption skyrocketed by 154%, yet searches for "occupational burnout" and "micro-retirement" highlight a growing work-life crisis.

Culturally, the shift is fascinating: spirituality has gone high-tech with Mahabharat AI, while social lives are moving toward "aesthetic outings" and the viral bhajan clubbing (up 26.9K%). From Gen Alpha’s parental controls to senior citizens seeking tech for aging, India is searching for ways to use bots to boost productivity while reclaiming physical joy through "fake weddings" and hiking trails.

**The End of the IPO Honeymoon**

The era of effortless "listing gains" is fading. After the post-2020 frenzy, the IPO market is cooling as one-year average returns plummeted to just 3.03% in 2025—a sharp reality check for retail investors. In 2024, only 4 companies were trading above their issue price a year later, compared to 52 in 2021.

With ₹1.75 trillion in the pipeline, the market is shifting from "liquidity-driven" to "fundamental-led." Geopolitical tensions and tighter secondary markets mean fewer multi-baggers and more "wait-and-watch" caution. For the modern investor, the lesson is clear: the "easy money" phase is over; now, only quality and valuation discipline will survive the listing day.

**INDIA NUCLEAR PROGRAM: KALPAKKAM FBR MILESTONE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• India’s 500 MWe Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR) at Kalpakkam reaches critical milestone  

**WHAT IT MEANS**  
• Part of India’s 3-stage nuclear program (Uranium → Plutonium → Thorium)  
• FBRs can “breed” more fissile material than they consume  
• Key step toward long-term thorium-based energy strategy  

**REAL STRATEGIC VALUE**  
• Reduces dependence on imported uranium over time  
• Enables utilisation of India’s large thorium reserves  
• Strengthens long-term energy security  

**IMPORTANT REALITY CHECK**  
• Not “infinite energy” – still requires complex fuel cycle & infrastructure  
• Thorium cycle (U-233) is technologically challenging & not yet fully commercialised at scale  
• Multiple reactors & decades of deployment needed for meaningful share  

**GLOBAL CONTEXT**  
• Russia operates commercial FBRs; others remain limited/prototype stage  
• India among few nations pursuing full thorium roadmap  

**LONG-TERM OUTLOOK**  
• Potential to support a meaningful share of power mix over decades  
• Clean, baseload power with strategic independence benefits  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Long-term) – Major step in nuclear self-reliance, but gradual execution required

Ridham Desai

BROKERAGE RADAR

Expect revenue across coverage to grow in the high single digits, while profits are likely to rise only in the low single digits due to margin compression

India, however, continues to transition into a higher earnings growth phase, supported by sustained policy stimulus

Nine sectors are expected to register positive revenue growth, with Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services leading the pack

Utilities is set to report a de-growth in revenue

Growth in earnings is likely to be led by Communication Services, Materials and Consumer Discretionary

Energy and Gas Utilities are expected to report a decline in earnings

Bharti Airtel and Tata Steel should be the biggest contributors to aggregate BSE Sensex earnings

SBI is expected to be the poorest performer

45% of our coverage to see margin expansion

Margins are expected to rise for three out of 10 sectors

Utilities and Technology are likely to show expansion, whereas margins for Energy and Financials are likely to fall the most

Industry at a “breaking point”, with uncertainty outweighing immediate income concerns

**China’s Export Resilience**

​The Middle East energy crisis is proving to be an unexpected windfall for China’s export juggernaut. While rivals struggle with skyrocketing fuel costs and supply chain shocks, China is leveraging its massive oil reserves and domestic energy supply to maintain production. This "supply-side resilience" is even reversing "China plus one" trends, as global clients prioritize stability over diversification.

​However, it’s not all smooth sailing. The conflict brings "imported inflation" that could squeeze already thin manufacturing margins, and global demand remains a wildcard. If the war persists, China is well-positioned to snatch more market share, further cementing its role as the world’s most reliable—and cushioned—factory floor.

**Earnings: The Market’s North Star**

Forty years of Sensex history reveal a humbling truth: arithmetic trumps stories. While sentiment and "re-rating" can spark short-term fireworks, long-term wealth is almost exclusively fueled by corporate earnings growth. Over most rolling 10-year periods, Sensex returns have mirrored earnings at a steady 10-12%.

The "myth of extraordinary returns" often stems from rare windows where earnings and valuation multiples expand simultaneously. Today, we are in an earnings-led phase where valuations are already demanding. The disciplined takeaway? Stop hunting for spectacular "re-rating" stories and bet on the quiet, durable growth of bottom-line profits.

Grid-Scale Transformer Lessons

India’s chaotic electricity demand is the ultimate "stress test" for AI. A new study shows that Temporal Fusion Transformers outperformed deeper models by effectively juggling historical data, calendar variables, and static metadata—achieving a 4.15% error rate despite unmetered agricultural loads and shifting monsoon patterns.

The lesson? Domain fit beats architectural hype. While sophisticated models failed, the transformer’s ability to "weigh" which inputs matter most proved that India’s grid complexity requires specialized reasoning, not just raw power. However, better forecasts are useless without the regulatory flexibility to act on them.

- Expect a **gradual recovery trajectory**, but **growth trends stay uneven**, supporting Goldman Sachs’ **Neutral stance** with a **target price of ₹490**.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON MARICO: Strong Growth & Margin Tailwinds | TP ₹860 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Revenue growth above 20% YoY**, showcasing **robust operating momentum**.  
- **India volumes** in **high single digits**, indicating **steady demand trends**.  
- **Value-added hair oils** growing **above 20%**, sustaining strong traction.  

**Margins & Segment Trends**  
- **Copra prices down ~35%**, driving **gross margin expansion**.  
- **Foods and premium personal care** businesses showing **encouraging recovery**.  
- **International business** maintains **high-teens growth** in constant currency terms.  

**Outlook**  
- **Volume recovery** and **margin tailwinds** underpin a **positive growth outlook**, prompting Goldman Sachs to **maintain Buy** with a **target price of ₹860**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON TATA MOTORS PV: Demand Weakness Persists | TP ₹340 | Equal-Weight**

**Key Highlights**  
- **March Land Rover sales** down **16% YoY (8,100 units)**, though slightly improved vs February.  
- **Incentives surged 26% YoY**, signaling **soft demand conditions**.  
- **Q4 sales down 21% YoY (21,000 units)**, **flat QoQ** despite steeper discounts.  

**Regional Trends**  
- Including **Jaguar**, **US retail sales down 22% YoY (22,100 units)**.  
- **Incentives up 27% YoY**, reflecting **continued pressure on demand**.  

**Outlook**  
- **Wholesale volumes** likely remain **strong**, but **retail momentum** continues to **lag**, prompting Morgan Stanley to **maintain Equal-Weight** with a **target price of ₹340**.

**ELARA CAPITAL ON US PHARMA TARIFFS: Generics Exempt, Innovators at Risk**

**Key Highlights**  
- **US proposes 100% tariffs** on **patented drugs**, while **generics remain exempt** for now.  
- **Exemption for generics** is **positive for Indian pharma**, limiting near-term pressure.  
- **One-year review clause** adds **medium-term policy uncertainty**.  

**Sector Impact**  
- **Innovator companies** face potential risk, though **mitigating factors** exist.  
- **Orphan drugs** remain **exempt**, supporting niche portfolios.  

**Outlook**  
- **Near-term impact appears limited**, but **policy-driven risks** may resurface over the medium term.

**CITI ON BANK OF BARODA: Strong Growth Momentum | TP ₹360 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**  
- **Sequential advances growth >6%**, coming in **ahead of estimates**.  
- **Overseas advances** remain **resilient** despite ongoing **geopolitical tensions**.  
- **Q4 earnings** likely to benefit from **higher recoveries** on written-off accounts.  
- **Income tax refund** expected to **lower effective tax rate**, further supporting profitability.  

**Outlook**  
- Citi maintains a **Buy rating** with a **target price of ₹360**, citing strong operational momentum and improving earnings outlook.

**CITI ON PNB: Growth Above Guidance But Valuation Concerns | TP ₹115 | Sell**

**Key Highlights**

- **Gross advances** slightly **ahead of estimates**, indicating steady momentum.  
- **RAM segment (Retail, Agriculture, MSME)** expected to post **mid-teen growth**.  
- **Undisbursed sanction line of ₹1.02 lakh crore** to aid **corporate credit growth**.  
- Bank has **exceeded its loan growth guidance** of 11–12%, but **valuation remains a concern** prompting a **Sell rating**.

**CITI ON M&M FINANCE: Strong Growth Momentum Ahead | TP ₹425 | Buy**

**Key Highlights**

- **Disbursements and asset growth** gaining traction, supported by seasonal factors.  
- **Tractor and CV segments** driving overall growth performance.  
- **NIMs expected to moderate** as benefits from lower funding costs ease.  
- Forecast for **Q4:** **ROA at 2.5%**, **ROE at 15%**, indicating solid profitability.

**HSBC ON CABLES & WIRES: Strong Q4 Growth Despite Margin Pressure**

**Key Highlights**

- **Revenue growth robust in Q4**, led mainly by higher realisations.  
- **Profitability impacted** due to rising input costs and lower operating leverage.  
- **West Asia disruptions** weighing on **near-term earnings visibility**.  
- **Ratings:** Maintain **Buy** on **Polycab** and **RR Kabel**; **Hold** on **KEI Industries**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON NMDC: Iron Ore Prices Surge**

**Key Highlights**

- **NMDC raises iron ore prices sharply**, backed by firm global trends and robust domestic steel demand.  
- **Strong restocking demand** continues to support healthy volume growth.  
- **China’s supply reforms** and rising shipments from **Guinea’s Simandou** may pose **downside risks** ahead.  
- **West Asia developments** remain a key **near-term monitor** for price stability.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON DMART: Upgraded to Overweight | TP ₹5,188**

**Key Highlights**

- **Q4 revenue growth at 19%**, well above recent trend.  
- **Faster store expansion** and **moderate inflation** seen as major tailwinds.  
- **Positioned as a defensive bet** within the discretionary sector.  
- **Revenue growth outlook** strong — expected to improve to **~19–20% in FY27–28**.

**MORGAN STANLEY ON BANKS & NBFCs: Growth Momentum Strengthens in 4QFY26**

**Key Highlights**

- Loan and deposit growth **accelerated YoY** across lenders, signaling improving momentum versus 3Q.  
- **4Q seasonality** aided stronger growth; system data tracking **ahead of expectations**.  
- **NBFCs** saw solid traction — **Bajaj Finance AUM up 22%+ YoY**, **L&T Finance retail loans up ~26% YoY**.  
- **HDFC Bank’s LDR** fell ~400bps QoQ, indicating robust deposit inflows.  
- **Asset quality** stable to improving across most lenders.  
- Focus now shifting to **FY27 outlook**, with attention on macro and geopolitical headwinds.

**GOLDMAN SACHS ON INDIA RETAIL: Inflation Risks Rising for FY27**

**Key Takeaways**

- Inflation risk expected to rise into 1HFY27, likely to weigh on discretionary demand.  
- Crude-linked raw materials (polyester, PU, EVA) trending higher — signaling margin pressure from 1QFY27.  
- **Metro Brands** remains **preferred pick** for its premium focus and strong ability to pass on higher costs.  
- **Page Industries** to benefit from ~2% price hike, full effect visible from 1QFY27.  
- **Trent** faces limited pricing power in value segment (**Zudio**), constraining margin recovery.  
- **Bata India** maintained at **Sell** amid weaker brand positioning and mounting cost pressures.

**BANK OF INDIA: MORGAN STANLEY MAINTAINS UNDERWEIGHT DESPITE STRONG GROWTH**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Morgan Stanley maintains Underweight with target price of ₹120, even as balance sheet growth and RAM segment remain strong  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: Underweight  
• Target Price: ₹120  

**Q4 BUSINESS UPDATE**  
• Strong balance sheet growth momentum  
• Domestic deposit growth remains robust  
• Continued traction in key lending segments  

**KEY BUSINESS DRIVERS**  

• Deposit Growth:  
  – Healthy domestic deposit growth supporting funding base  
  – Strengthens liability franchise  

• RAM Segment Strength:  
  – Strong growth in RAM (Retail, Agriculture, MSME)  
  – Key driver for credit expansion and diversification  

• Balance Sheet Expansion:  
  – Overall growth remains healthy across segments  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Strong operational momentum led by RAM and deposits  
• Diversified loan book improving stability  
• However, concerns likely around valuation, margins, or asset quality  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral – Strong growth fundamentals offset by cautious stance from Morgan Stanley

**DMART: CLSA HIGH-CONVICTION OUTPERFORM WITH STRONG GROWTH MOMENTUM**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• CLSA reiterates high-conviction outperform with target price of ₹6583, driven by strong sales growth, store expansion, and improving pricing environment  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: High-Conviction Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹6583  

**OPERATIONAL PERFORMANCE**  
• Revenue: ₹17,205 Cr — modest beat vs estimates  
• Sales Growth: ↑ 18.9% YoY, showing clear QoQ acceleration  

**KEY BUSINESS DRIVERS**  

• Strong Demand Momentum:  
  – Healthy traction across categories driving revenue growth  
  – Acceleration indicates improving consumption trend  

• Inflation Tailwind:  
  – ~3% inflation supporting pricing and realizations  
  – Aids topline growth without hurting demand  

• Aggressive Store Expansion:  
  – 58+ store additions  
  – Total store network reaches 500  
  – Strong footprint expansion driving future growth visibility  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Scale advantage with continued store addition strategy  
• Inflation aiding realizations in value retail model  
• Consistent execution driving steady growth  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong growth visibility supported by store expansion, improving demand, and favorable pricing environment

**TVS MOTOR: GOLDMAN SACHS REITERATES BUY, RAISES TARGET PRICE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Goldman Sachs maintains BUY with increased target price of ₹4100 (earlier ₹3830), citing strong pricing power and premium-led growth visibility  

**RATING & TARGET UPDATE**  
• Recommendation: Buy  
• Target Price: ₹4100  
• Earlier Target: ₹3830 (revised upward)  

**KEY INVESTMENT RATIONALE**  

• Strong Pricing Power:  
  – Ability to pass on input cost increases effectively  
  – Better margin protection vs peers  

• Superior Volume Visibility:  
  – Driven by upcoming premium product launches  
  – Strong positioning in high-growth segments  

• RM Inflation Cushion:  
  – Better placed to offset raw material cost pressures  
  – Limited impact from metal & crude inflation on volumes  

• Margin Tailwinds:  
  – ~35 bps margin expansion expected from PLI scheme benefits  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAYS**  
• Premiumisation strategy driving both growth and margins  
• Strong competitive positioning vs peers in current cycle  
• Policy support (PLI) adds incremental profitability  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong earnings visibility supported by pricing power, premium products, and structural margin tailwinds

Morgan Stanley On Financials

Q4 Initial Updates: Broadbased volume growth acceleration

Year-on-year loan growth accelerated for both banks and NBFCs

Year-on-year deposit growth accelerated for banks

Q4 is a seasonally strong quarter and sees back-ended growth, year-on-year growth accelerated notably

NBFCs that put out updates also saw higher year-on-year AUM growth vs. Q3

Bajaj Finance delivering >22% year-on-year, while L&T Finance saw 26% year-on-year in retail loans

These numbers were in general higher than estimates

HDFC Bank saw its loan-to-deposit ratio decline 400 bps quarter-on-quarter and 200 bps year-on-year as deposit growth outpaced loan growth

Asset quality updates put out by Mahindra Finance, L&T Finance and IDFC First Bank were also positive

Jefferies On Pharma

US Tariffs: Relief for Indian Generics; Sai Life most exposed CDMO

Major patented drug manufacturing hubs like Europe and Korea will face 15% tariffs

Companies with MFN deals and US investments will also be exempted

Among Indian firms, in a worst case scenario, estimate mid-single digit impact on FY27E EPS of Sun

Morgan Stanley On Iron Ore Prices Hike

NMDC increases iron ore prices sharply

See continued elevated seaborne iron ore prices

This is relatively negative for JSW Steel and Jindal Steel given their external iron ore requirements

China's steel supply reforms in play, coupled with higher shipments, should turn sentiment negative for seaborne iron prices

Key to watch is how the Middle-East situation evolves

Kotak Securities On Pharma

US tariff structure fundamentally pressurizes pharma companies

Need to relocate manufacturing to the US, negotiate MFN deals and form partnerships with those companies and countries that have secured favorable deals

Generics being excluded for now

SUN Pharma and CRDMOs are exposed to the risk

Assuming a nil tariff pass-through, expect a modest 1-2% EPS impact on SUN Pharma

The implications for innovator-focused CRDMOs are more nuanced
